title: erythraea maritima var. lutea
intro: Erythraea maritima var. lutea, also known as Marsh Rosemary, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. This shrubby perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Erythraea maritima var. lutea include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb for treating conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. Erythraea maritima var. lutea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote respiratory health and overall well-being.
